question: By what factor do members who complete their first transaction within 5 days of onboarding show lower first-year churn compared to members who take more than 14 days to transact?

top_k_facts.title: Time to First Transaction and Early-Cohort Churn Reduction
Governance Training Completion Target and Voting Multiplier

vanilla.answer: According to the reference facts, members who complete their first transaction within 5 days of onboarding show **67% lower first-year churn** compared to members who take more than 14 days to transact.

top_k_facts.title: Time to First Transaction and Early-Cohort Churn Reduction
Governance Training Completion Target and Voting Multiplier
Grievance Escalation Timeline and Trust Rating at High CHS

kp_on.answer:
According to the reference facts, members who complete their first transaction within 5 days of onboarding show **67% lower first-year churn** compared to members who take more than 14 days to transact.

To express this as a factor: if we denote the churn rate for the 14+ day group as the baseline, the 5-day group has churn that is 33% of the baseline (100% - 67% = 33%), meaning the churn rate is reduced by a factor of approximately **3x** (or more precisely, the 5-day group has 1/3 the churn rate of the 14+ day group).

However, the reference facts state the metric as a **67% reduction**, not as a multiplicative factor.

question: What NPS score range do cooperative platforms that are assessed as 'genuinely cooperative in governance' typically achieve?

top_k_facts.title: Member Satisfaction NPS Target and Genuinely-Cooperative Band

vanilla.answer: According to the reference facts, cooperative platforms assessed as 'genuinely cooperative in governance' typically achieve NPS scores in the **50-65 range**. This is identified as the sector benchmark for top-quartile cooperative governance, compared to the minimum certification threshold of 42.

top_k_facts.title: Member Satisfaction NPS Target and Genuinely-Cooperative Band

kp_on.answer:
Based on the reference facts, cooperative platforms assessed as 'genuinely cooperative in governance' typically achieve NPS scores in the **50-65 range**.

This is described as the sector benchmark for top-quartile cooperative governance, compared to the minimum certification threshold of 42.

question: By what factor are members who complete governance orientation training within their first 90 days more likely to vote in their first annual election?

top_k_facts.title: Governance Training Completion Target and Voting Multiplier
Time to First Transaction and Early-Cohort Churn Reduction

vanilla.answer: According to the reference facts, members who complete governance orientation training within their first 90 days are **3.4 times more likely to vote** in their first annual election compared to members who do not complete the training.

top_k_facts.title: Governance Training Completion Target and Voting Multiplier
Time to First Transaction and Early-Cohort Churn Reduction
Account Inactivity Warning Threshold and AI Governance Participation Multiplier

kp_on.answer:
According to the reference facts, members who complete governance orientation training within their first 90 days are **3.4 times more likely** to vote in their first annual election than members who do not complete the training.

This is stated in fact [1] (MJ-16: Governance Training Completion Target and Voting Multiplier) as the "Governance Training Voting Multiplier."

question: Under the Cooperative Capital Framework's tax compliance guidance, what minimum percentage of patronage distributions must be paid in cash within the current tax year to qualify for qualified-notice tax treatment?

top_k_facts.title: Patronage Statement Delivery Timeline and Cash Payment Requirement

vanilla.answer: According to the reference facts, under the Cooperative Capital Framework's tax compliance guidance, **20%** of patronage distributions must be paid in cash within the current tax year to qualify for qualified-notice tax treatment.

top_k_facts.title: Patronage Statement Delivery Timeline and Cash Payment Requirement

kp_on.answer: According to the Cooperative Capital Framework's tax compliance guidance (MJ-19), **20%** of patronage distributions must be paid in cash within the current tax year for the distribution to qualify for qualified-notice tax treatment.

question: What surplus reserve threshold must be exceeded before a cooperative platform can distribute profits to its member-owners?

top_k_facts.title: Member Satisfaction NPS Target and Genuinely-Cooperative Band

vanilla.answer:
Based on the provided reference facts, a cooperative platform can distribute profits to its member-owners when reserves reach **110% of the reserve floor**.

This is specified in **EG-20: Surplus Distribution Trigger Threshold**, which states that 110% of the reserve floor triggers surplus distribution.

top_k_facts.title: Member Satisfaction NPS Target and Genuinely-Cooperative Band
Time to First Transaction and Early-Cohort Churn Reduction

kp_on.answer:
According to the reference facts, a cooperative platform can distribute surplus to member-owners when reserves reach **110% of the reserve floor**.

This is specified in **EG-20: Surplus Distribution Trigger Threshold**. The surplus distribution is triggered at 110% of the reserve floor, ensuring that the reserve base is adequately secured before profits are redirected to members.

question: What is the canonical amendment supermajority threshold required for constitutional changes in the Verdania cooperative framework?

vanilla.answer: According to the reference facts, the canonical amendment supermajority threshold required for constitutional changes in the Verdania cooperative framework is **66.7%** (CS-03: Amendment Supermajority Threshold).

top_k_facts.title: Account Inactivity Warning Threshold and AI Governance Participation Multiplier

kp_on.answer:
According to the reference facts, the canonical amendment supermajority threshold required for constitutional changes in the Verdania cooperative framework is **66.7%**.

This is specified in CS-03: Amendment Supermajority Threshold, which notes that this supermajority threshold is calibrated to protect governance resilience by preventing transient majorities from rewriting foundational agreements while avoiding entrenchment of outdated provisions.
